titlepage.titleproper: Guide to the Frederick George Marcham Papers,1925-1993.
titlepage.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections Cornell University Library

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu
http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

titlepage.date: © 2003 Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library

did.head: DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY
did.physdesc: Correspondence, minutes, manuscripts, articles, course outlines, notebooks, files, an audiotape, passports, a diploma, account books, legal papers, and other materials.
did.repository: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library
did.abstract: Papers related to Marcham's work as Professor of English History at Cornell University.

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Professor of English history, Cornell University.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Largely correspondence containing recommendations for former students and requests for such. Other letters deal with university affairs, publication of Marcham's books by MacMillan and Harper's, requests by publishing houses and scholarly journals for reader reports, and reports from students on their research progress. Includes some minutes of history department meetings, manuscripts of speeches and reports by Marcham and others, articles by Marcham on athletics, course outlines and notebooks, papers relating to history workshops offered at Cornell, items pertaining to the British Businessmen's Course (1948). Includes files pertaining to a program financed by the General Education Board intended to improve the level of teaching history in high schools, ca. 1943-1944, and files relating to a History Dept. program concerning teaching critical thinking, ca. 1940-1944. Also, drafts and working papers of his projected history of Cornell; "Essays on Oliver Cromwell" prepared by John Marcham; a tape recording of Marcham's memorial service. Also, passports, a diploma, account books, and legal papers.

did.unittitle: World War II newsletters and correspondence
Workshop on Latin America, the Far East and the British Commonwealth
Workshop on Latin America, the Far East and the British Commonwealth of Nations
Cornell University Curriculum Series in World History
"Essays on Oliver Cromwell" prepared by John Marcham
"Letters of an English Physician in the early Seventeenth Century", by F. G. Marcham
"Six short essays presenting my thoughts on myself as a member of my community" by F. G. Marcham
Speech for Mr. and Mrs. Cyrus Eaton re: Goldwin Smith
Oversize photograph of Prof. Marcham with group of British businessmen
Toward an Ivy Group Handbook for Intercollegiate Athletics

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 7-11, and 18: access restricted to permission of donor.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Frederick George Marcham papers, #14-17-603. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
